name: dvp-suspension-reject
description: Demonstrates the negative path. Same setup as `dvp-happy-path`, but the status_operator suspends one counterparty between trades. When the second trade attempts to settle through the wrapper, the on-chain compliance gate rejects with the distinct `COMPLIANCE_SUSPENDED` error code (0x2013) — not a generic transfer error. This is the consulting-value differentiator vs. a Token-2022 transfer hook alone: operators see *why* it failed.
headline: A suspended counterparty makes SettleDvp reject with COMPLIANCE_SUSPENDED (0x2013), not a generic error.

steps.explanation: Assume the `dvp-happy-path` scenario has already run successfully — registry + two verified participants exist.
Status operator suspends USER_B. Status moves from VERIFIED to SUSPENDED.
Verify the new status. Should return SUSPENDED.
Attempt to settle a fresh DvP. The wrapper's compliance check rejects with the distinct `COMPLIANCE_SUSPENDED` code instead of a generic transfer error.
(Optional) Restore the participant with `verify` to confirm the gate is symmetric and demonstrates the reversal path.
